what_it_does: Archives session 12c59b4e as a verbatim foundational exchange in which Tim introduces 'open-future mode' and the broader idea of modes, and records the response that locks the term and treats modes as a Layer 4 Company primitive.
observations: Verbatim reproduction of Tim's dictated message with TTS spelling/grammar artifacts preserved and bracketed readability corrections explicitly marked.
Tim coins 'open future mode' and expresses a wish to switch entities to needed modes rather than guide them.
The response canonicalizes 'open-future mode' and retires alternative candidate names (seed-tree, tributary, iceberg, etc.).
Frames modes as a general Company architectural primitive distinct from roles or departments.
Connects Company modes to Vi's activity-state modes via the Universal Composition pattern.
Lists insights: switching-cost asymmetry, modes stack, manual and inferred entry triggers, mode discovery, and mode definition as Company capabilities.
Explicitly defers designing the actual mode mechanism (entry/exit, stacking, discovery, definition).
standouts: 'Open-future mode' is locked in as canonical vocabulary.
Modes are proposed as a Company-wide operational primitive separate from roles/departments.
Mode-switching is framed as an AI affordance because it is near-instant compared with human switching cost.
conventions: Follows exchange frontmatter with type, sequence, title, date, session, verbatim flag, produced list, and tags.
Uses [[wikilink]] references for produced items and source index.
Includes a primary-source info box explaining verbatim handling.
Preserves dictated errors and marks bracketed corrections as added text.
Separates verbatim message, response, analysis, recording decisions, produced items, and source.
concerns: The file is intentionally speculative: it records a primitive and a named mode without defining mechanism, so readers must not treat it as a designed system.
Bracketed corrections could make it slightly harder to recover the exact original wording even though the convention is explained.
notes: Primary-source exchange that canonizes 'open-future mode' and seeds modes as a Company primitive while deliberately leaving the mechanism undefined.
questions: What exact criteria determine when a mode becomes canonical?
How will Company modes technically relate to Vi's ~19 activity-state modes?
What is the full set of candidate modes and their precise definitions?
What does 'Layer 4' specifically represent in this project's layer model?
purpose_vs_actual: Stated purpose is to reproduce Tim's verbatim message and record resulting decisions; the file does exactly that, plus adds interpretive analysis and open questions.
apparent_themes: Modes as a reusable operational primitive
Friction elimination through switchability rather than guidance
AI affordances versus human cognitive limits
Distinction between identity/role and operational mode
intention_signals: Capture a founder's spontaneous concept ('open-future mode'), formalize it as canonical vocabulary, and elevate 'modes' into a reusable Company design primitive without prematurely architecting the mechanism.
novelty: Treats a writing style ('open-future') as a formal mode and frames mode-switching as an AI affordance rather than a human process, distinguishing modes from roles/departments.
contribution: Provides a named, locked term ('open-future mode') and a conceptual frame (modes as distinct from roles, stackable, manually/auto-triggered, discoverable, definable) that a fused system can reference when building a mode system.
summary_for_embedding: Foundational exchange 08 records Tim's verbatim introduction of 'open-future mode' and the broader idea of modes, canonicalizes the term, and frames modes as a reusable Company primitive distinct from roles, with connections to Vi activity-state modes.
intention_for_embedding: To preserve the primary-source moment that named and locked 'open-future mode' and seeded modes as a cross-layer architectural primitive while explicitly deferring mechanism design.
